key_insight: Compositional internal structure emerges only in a narrow depth-and-connectivity sweet spot, and outside that regime gradient descent tends to find fractured, non-compositional solutions.
engineering_relevance: For solid mechanics and fracture/damage modeling, this suggests network architecture choice can be as important as training for recovering reusable physical primitives and avoiding brittle learned representations.
method: Similarity-based pruning (SP) to recover compositional connectivity patterns
Heuristic depth predictor to estimate depths where compositionality is most likely
Theoretical explanation using compositional sparsity, volume-ratio arguments, and feature-interference bounds
evidence_signal: The abstract reports empirical tests across varying sparsity/connectivity and depth, showing compositionality peaks only in specific target-dependent depth ranges and depends on which connections remain, not sparsity alone.
watch_item: The benefits appear highly architecture- and target-dependent, so the pruning and depth prediction heuristics may not transfer reliably without careful tuning and validation.
paper_title: Compositionality Emerges in a Narrow Depth-Connectivity Regime: Architecture Constraints and Solution Manifolds
